title: acciddasuite
text: acciddasuite package provides tools building infectious disease forecasts…? vignette demonstrates standard frameworks create evaluate forecasts using R. aim demonstrate basic steps forecasting task, defined Hyndman & Athanasopoulos (2021).

title: acciddasuite
text: Ideally, load data . demonstration purposes, load data CDC National Health Safety Network. data dictionary available .
code: library(dplyr) library(ggplot2) library(pipetime) library(fabletools) library(acciddasuite) df <- get_data(pathogen = "covid", geo_values = "ny") summary(df) #>      as_of              location            target          #>  Min.   :2026-01-11   Length:284         Length:284         #>  1st Qu.:2026-01-11   Class :character   Class :character   #>  Median :2026-01-11   Mode  :character   Mode  :character   #>  Mean   :2026-01-11                                         #>  3rd Qu.:2026-01-11                                         #>  Max.   :2026-01-11                                         #>  target_end_date       observation      #>  Min.   :2020-08-08   Min.   :   60.0   #>  1st Qu.:2021-12-16   1st Qu.:  463.5   #>  Median :2023-04-25   Median : 1005.5   #>  Mean   :2023-04-25   Mean   : 1653.0   #>  3rd Qu.:2024-09-01   3rd Qu.: 2254.2   #>  Max.   :2026-01-10   Max.   :11833.0

title: acciddasuite
text: evaluate predictive performance, employ time series cross-validation. fit models using data available specific cutoff point (eval_start_date), forecast h weeks ahead expanding windows. want eval_start_date far back time can computationally expensive. visualise data decide eval_start_date.  Default models :  Naïve (Random Walk RW): baseline model carrying forward last observation.ETS: Exponential Smoothing state space model (automatically selected). ARIMA: Auto-Regressive Integrated Moving Average model (automatically selected).
code: # We ony evaluate on the last 30 days of data for demonstration purposes eval_start_date <- max(df$target_end_date) - 30  df |>   ggplot(aes(x = target_end_date, y = observation)) +   geom_line() +   geom_vline(     xintercept = eval_start_date,     linetype = "dashed",     color = "red"   ) +   annotate(     "label",     x = eval_start_date,     y = max(df$observation) * 0.8,     label = "Model evaluation\nstarts here",     color = "red"   ) +   scale_x_date(date_labels = "%b\n%y", breaks = "5 months") +   theme_classic() fcast = get_fcast(   df,   eval_start_date = eval_start_date,   top_n = 4, # Select top 4 models   h = 4 # forecast 4 weeks ahead ) |>   time_pipe("forecasting") #> Time Series Cross Validation... #> Generating final forecasts...  fcast #> <accida_cast> #>  #> Models evaluated: #> # A tibble: 5 × 2 #>   .model    CRPS #>   <chr>    <dbl> #> 1 ETS       44.6 #> 2 ARIMA     46.1 #> 3 ENSEMBLE  58.4 #> 4 THETA     65.9 #> 5 SNAIVE   248.  #>  #> Forecast horizon: #>   From: 2026-01-17  #>   To:   2026-02-07  #>  #> Contents: #>   $forecast  fable forecast object #>   $score     model ranking table #>   $plot      ggplot2 object fcast$plot

title: acciddasuite
text: Example using EspiEstim projections.
code: extra <- list(   CUSTOM_ARIMA = ARIMA(observation ~ pdq(1,1,0)),   PROPHET = fable.prophet::prophet(observation ~ season("yearly"))   #EPIESTIM = ... )  fcast = get_fcast(   df,   eval_start_date = eval_start_date,   top_n = 4, # Select top 4 models   h = 4, # forecast 4 weeks ahead,   extra_models = extra ) |>   time_pipe("forecasting")

title: Get hospitalisation data — get_data
text: Fetch recent confirmed US hospital admissions NHSN source COVID-19, influenza, RSV using pub_covidcast.

title: Get hospitalisation data — get_data
text: pathogen Character. One "covid", "flu" "rsv". geo_values Character vector. Geographic values per pub_covidcast filter . Defaults "*" (states).

title: Get hospitalisation data — get_data
text: tibble hubverse format columns: as_of: Date data issued location: State code target: Target description (e.g., "wk inc covid hosp") target_end_date: End date target week observation: Observed hospital admissions

title: Get hospitalisation data — get_data
code: get_data(pathogen = "covid", geo_values = c("ca", "ny")) #> # A tibble: 568 × 5 #>    as_of      location target            target_end_date observation #>    <date>     <chr>    <chr>             <date>                <dbl> #>  1 2026-01-11 CA       wk inc covid hosp 2020-08-08             4836 #>  2 2026-01-11 NY       wk inc covid hosp 2020-08-08              517 #>  3 2026-01-11 CA       wk inc covid hosp 2020-08-15             4273 #>  4 2026-01-11 NY       wk inc covid hosp 2020-08-15              490 #>  5 2026-01-11 CA       wk inc covid hosp 2020-08-22             3498 #>  6 2026-01-11 NY       wk inc covid hosp 2020-08-22              844 #>  7 2026-01-11 CA       wk inc covid hosp 2020-08-29             3100 #>  8 2026-01-11 NY       wk inc covid hosp 2020-08-29              483 #>  9 2026-01-11 CA       wk inc covid hosp 2020-09-05             2888 #> 10 2026-01-11 NY       wk inc covid hosp 2020-09-05              479 #> # ℹ 558 more rows

what: Rolling origin epidemic forecasts and model evaluation — get_fcast
title: Rolling origin epidemic forecasts and model evaluation — get_fcast
text: Fits multiple time series models weekly incidence data evaluates short term predictive performance using expanding window rolling origin scheme.

title: Rolling origin epidemic forecasts and model evaluation — get_fcast
code: get_fcast(df, eval_start_date, h = 4, top_n = 3, extra_models = NULL)

title: Rolling origin epidemic forecasts and model evaluation — get_fcast
text: df Data frame weekly observations containing target_end_date (Date) observation (numeric). eval_start_date Date string coercible Date. First date forecasts evaluated. least 52 weeks data must precede date. h Integer. Forecast horizon weeks. Default 4. top_n Integer. Number top ranked models included ensemble. Default 3. extra_models Named list additional forecasting models passed fabletools::model(). element must reference observation column compatible fabletools::forecast(). Custom models can constructed using fable modelling framework, see fabletools extension_models vignette. name list element used model label output.

title: Rolling origin epidemic forecasts and model evaluation — get_fcast
text: object class accida_cast containing: forecast Final h week ahead forecasts models ensemble. score Model ranking based rolling origin CRPS. plot ggplot object showing forecasts prediction intervals.

title: Rolling origin epidemic forecasts and model evaluation — get_fcast
text: eval_start_date onwards, models repeatedly refitted data available evaluation time point used forecast next h weeks. Forecasts compared observations using Continuous Ranked Probability Score (CRPS). Models ranked mean CRPS across evaluation periods. best performing top_n models combined equal weight ensemble. final h week ahead forecast produced refitting selected models using full dataset.

title: Rolling origin epidemic forecasts and model evaluation — get_fcast
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ extra_models <- list(   CUSTOM_ARIMA = ARIMA(observation ~ pdq(1, 1, 0)),   PROPHET = fable.prophet::PROPHET(observation) )  get_fcast(   df,   eval_start_date = "2025-01-01",   h = 4,   top_n = 3,   extra_models = extra_models ) } # }
